description: Inputs with integers exceeding JavaScript safe integer range (Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER = 2^53 - 1). A JSON.parse-based emit silently produces imprecise values for large integers. The atomic emit parses digit-by-digit and may produce imprecise results for very large values — this class tests whether that imprecision is a shape-escape or a contained result.

inputs.label: max-safe-plus-one
inputs.rationale: 2^53 — beyond MAX_SAFE_INTEGER. Both arms likely produce imprecise result. Test if any arm crashes or throws unexpectedly vs silently loses precision. BENIGN-PASS because the spec does not declare a max-int constraint.

inputs.label: very-large-integer
inputs.rationale: Huge integer — well beyond safe range; both arms produce Infinity or imprecise value. BENIGN-PASS because input shape is valid (digits only).

inputs.label: zero-integer
inputs.rationale: Zero — valid non-negative integer boundary; should parse cleanly

inputs.label: leading-zeros
inputs.rationale: Leading zeros are not valid in the spec's integer grammar per strict decimal notation.

inputs.label: max-safe-exact
inputs.rationale: MAX_SAFE_INTEGER exactly — should be representable precisely in both arms

inputs.label: overflow-in-list
inputs.rationale: Large integer as second element; valid digit sequence — BENIGN-PASS despite precision loss

inputs.label: hex-notation
inputs.rationale: Hex prefix '0x' — 'x' is not a digit; SyntaxError after the leading '0'

inputs.label: octal-notation
inputs.rationale: Octal prefix '0o' — 'o' is not a digit; SyntaxError after the leading '0'

inputs.label: binary-notation
inputs.rationale: Binary prefix '0b' — 'b' is not a digit; SyntaxError after the leading '0'

inputs.label: plus-prefix-integer
inputs.rationale: '+' prefix is not a digit and not allowed in the spec's grammar

inputs.label: single-large-digit
inputs.rationale: Single digit 9 — valid, well within safe integer range
